Summary: Systematic elaboration. PAX5 is essential for B cell development in mice and determines B cell commitment and differentiation by activating the B cell–specific signaling proteins CD19 and BLNK. PAX5 is critical for distal V_H→D_HJ_H recombination in pro-B cells during early B cell development and it is essential for distal V_H gene rearrangement in pro‑B cells. After successful D_HJ_H recombination, EBF1 targets and activates PAX5 expression. After activating PAX5 expression, EBF1 subsequently blocks the activation of non-B-lineage genes. PAX5 and EBF1 cooperate to regulate MYC expression. PAX5 promotes distal V_H→D_HJ_H recombination by activating chromatin and inducing antisense transcription at PAX5‑activated intergenic repeat (PAIR) elements in the distal V_H cluster. Reintroduction of PAX5 into PAX5 knockout pro‑B cells induces large‑scale contraction of the Igh locus. In PAX5‑deficient mice B cells are severely blocked at the pro‑B stage and PAX5‑deleted mature B cells purified from the lymph nodes of CD19‑cre PAX5 fl/– Eμ‑BCL2 mice dedifferentiated into functional T cells after transplantation into RAG2 KO mice. Lesions in EBF1, PAX5, or MYC may drive B‑cell malignancy and induce B‑cell lymphomas.
Reference: Li, L., Zhang, D., & Cao, X. (2024). EBF1, PAX5, and MYC: regulation on B cell development and association with hematologic neoplasms. Front Immunol, 15, 1320689. https://doi.org/10.3389/fimmu.2024.1320689

Summary: Pax5 mediates lineage commitment in B cell progenitors. SIRT7 coordinates B cell development by deacetylating Pax5 at K198, which promotes Pax5 protein stability and transcriptional activity. Dynamic acetylation/deacetylation of Pax5 at K198 was required for B cell development and lineage restriction in the described transplantation of Pax5−/− pro-B cells expressing Pax5 constructs, and Pax5 WT restored donor-derived CD45.2+ B220+ CD19+ B cells in bone marrow while Pax5 K198Q and K198R did not. In Pax5−/− pro-B cells neither constitutive deacetylated nor acetylated Pax5 mimics rescued B cell differentiation, indicating that B cell development requires dynamic Pax5 deacetylation. A Pax5 K198 deacetylation mimic restored lineage commitment in Pax5−/− pro-B cells and rescued B cell differentiation in Sirt7−/− pro-B cells, suggesting an uncoupling of differentiation from lineage commitment. Pax5 K198R rescued defective B cell development in Sirt7−/− pro-B cells; Pax5 WT expression also reversed the reduction in B220+ CD19+ GFP+ B cells. Sirt7−/− pro-B cells produced donor-derived TCRβ+ T cells and NKP46+ NK cells in spleen. Sorted mature Sirt7−/− splenic B cells retained CD19 and did not yield T cells, indicating SIRT7 is required for establishment but not maintenance of B cell commitment. The SIRT7–Pax5 interplay was conserved in B cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia where SIRT7 expression correlated with good prognosis.
Reference: Gamez-Garcia, A., Espinosa-Alcantud, M., Bueno-Costa, A., Alari-Pahissa, E., Marazuela-Duque, A., Thackray, J. K., Ray, C., Berenguer, C., Kumari, P., Bech, J. J., Braun, T., Ianni, A., Tischfield, J. A., Serrano, L., Esteller, M., Sardina, J. L., De La Torre, C., Sigvardsson, M., Vazquez, B. N., & Vaquero, A. (2024). A SIRT7-dependent acetylation switch regulates early B cell differentiation and lineage commitment through Pax5. Nat Immunol, 25(12), 2308-2319. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41590-024-01995-7

Summary: PAX5 is a B-cell–specific activator that functions as a positive transcription factor for GINS1 expression in B lymphopoiesis and in B cells. Two putative PAX5 binding sites were identified in the human GINS1 promoter and PAX5 binding activates GINS1 transcription. Coordinated PAX5 and GINS1 expression occurred in mouse B cells under physiological and LPS-stimulated conditions and in human DLBCL cell lines under differentiation. Dysregulated PAX5 was linked to increased GINS1 and DLBCL progression, and circ1857 generated from PAX5 pre-mRNA stabilizes GINS1 mRNA, modulates GINS1 expression and promotes lymphoma progression.
Reference: Wang, T., Chen, Z., Li, C., Zhang, W., Huang, W., Xue, J., Wang, J., & Li, S. (2023). PAX5 and circ1857 affected DLBCL progression and B-cell proliferation through regulating GINS1. Cancer Sci, 114(8), 3203-3215. https://doi.org/10.1111/cas.15856

Summary: Pax5 is an essential transcription factor for B cell development. Expression of mouse Pax5 is regulated by multiple transcription factors and DNA-binding proteins, including PU.1, IRF4, IRF8, NF-κB, and EBF1. In a chicken mature B cell line (DT40), Thy28 binds the Pax5 promoter in a B cell–specific manner and positively regulates Pax5 expression. In Thy28-deficient mice, B cell development was normal and Pax5 expression in primary B cells was comparable to wild-type. Thus species-specific mechanisms regulate Pax5 expression and B cell development.
Reference: Kitaura, F., Yuno, M., Fujita, T., Wakana, S., Ueda, J., Yamagata, K., & Fujii, H. (2019). Normal B cell development and Pax5 expression in Thy28/ThyN1-deficient mice. PLoS One, 14(7), e0220199. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0220199

Summary:
Systematic elaboration.

PAX5 promotes differentiation and survival of mature B cells. In germinal center B cells PAX5 is predominantly SUMO1-conjugated at K257. SENP1 binds PAX5 and mediates deSUMOylation of PAX5 via its catalytic activity at Cys603. SENP1 deficiency increases SUMO1-conjugated PAX5 in induced germinal center B cells. The observation that SENP1 deficiency reduces PAX5 protein in germinal center B cells suggests that deSUMOylation influences PAX5 stability. SUMOylation of PAX5 suppresses AID expression and class-switch recombination. Blocking PAX5 SUMOylation with the K257R mutant increases AID expression in primary B cells and increases class-switching (IgG1+) in transferred B cells. Forced expression of PAX5 or the K257R mutant enhances class-switch recombination in stimulated B cells, and forced expression of PAX5 increases AID expression in SENP1-deficient B cells. Accordingly, SENP1 controls AID expression to regulate class-switch recombination through deSUMOylation of PAX5.
Reference: Qi, J., Yan, L., Sun, J., Huang, C., Su, B., Cheng, J., & Shen, L. (2024). SUMO-specific protease 1 regulates germinal center B cell response through deSUMOylation of PAX5. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A, 121(22), e2314619121. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2314619121
